key_insight: A post-trained 111B multilingual model can be efficiently adapted for Korean-English tool use by combining preamble-controlled reasoning with targeted supervised and reinforcement learning.
engineering_relevance: The work shows a practical path to deploy strong agentic reasoning on constrained hardware using 4-bit quantization and single-GPU serving while keeping Korean and English instruction quality intact.
method: Multilingual supervised fine-tuning for tool-use and instruction following
Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards for multi-step tasks and Korean response consistency
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved mathematical reasoning, function calling, and agentic NL2SQL performance, along with preserved general Korean and English instruction-following quality.
watch_item: Benefits may depend on carefully designed verifiable rewards and preamble control, and quantization could still introduce serving-time accuracy tradeoffs in some workflows.
paper_title: Think in English, Answer in Korean: Efficient Adaptation of Multilingual Tool-Using Agents
